protein_name: Component of the DNA-binding general RNA polymerase II transcription factor IID complex (TFIID). TFIID plays a critical role in the regulation of gene transcription in eukaryotic cells. Belongs to the TAF13 family. Note: This description may include information from UniProtKB.

raw_fields.Cellular Component: nucleolus; nucleoplasm; nucleus; transcription factor TFIID complex
raw_fields.Molecular Function: DNA binding; protein binding; protein heterodimerization activity; RNA polymerase II general transcription initiation factor activity; TBP-class protein binding
raw_fields.Biological Process: DNA-templated transcription initiation; mRNA transcription by RNA polymerase II; positive regulation of transcription initiation by RNA polymerase II; RNA polymerase II preinitiation complex assembly; transcription by RNA polymerase II; transcription initiation at RNA polymerase II promoter
raw_fields.Alt. Names/Synonyms: MGC22425; MRT60; TAF(II)18; TAF13; TAF13 RNA polymerase II, TATA box binding protein (TBP)-associated factor, 18kDa; TAF2K; TAFII-18; TAFII18; TATA box binding protein (TBP)-associated factor, RNA polymerase II, K, 18kD; TATA-box binding protein associated factor 13; transcription initiation factor TFIID 18 kD subunit; Transcription initiation factor TFIID 18 kDa subunit; Transcription initiation factor TFIID subunit 13
